codeReview.summary: Implementation follows agent-team-orchestration skill. Created AgentHandoff and AgentComment types with required fields (what_was_done, artifacts, verification, known_issues, whats_next) and comment types (Start, Blocker, Handoff, Completion). PlannerAgent and GeneratorAgent produce proper structured handoffs. EvaluatorAgent produces handoff only in non-stub mode.

issues.description: EvaluatorAgent in stub mode (validation_pipeline.is_none()) returns early WITHOUT including the structured handoff in output. The handoff is only added in the full validation path (~line 2104). Test acknowledges this at line 7305: '// In stub mode, output is plain EvaluationResult JSON // The handoff is only included in full validation mode'. The expected behavior states 'EvaluatorAgent produces completion handoff summary' but this only happens in non-stub mode.
Clippy warning: comparison is useless due to type limits. `assert!(review.score >= 0)` where score is f64 - comparison with 0 is always true.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature implementation introduces structured handoffs and agent comments as a new pattern, but AGENTS.md does not document this protocol. The architecture.md in .factory/library/ also doesn't mention handoffs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md defines agent types (planner, generator, evaluator) but doesn't specify the handoff format or comment types. Worker correctly followed agent-team-orchestration skill which defines handoff structure.

summary: Feature passes review. Implementation correctly follows agent-team-orchestration skill with AgentHandoff struct (what_was_done, artifacts, verification, known_issues, whats_next) and AgentComment types (Start/Blocker/Handoff/Completion). PlannerAgent and GeneratorAgent produce proper structured handoffs. Non-blocking issue: EvaluatorAgent only produces handoff in non-stub mode; stub mode returns early with plain EvaluationResult. Minor clippy warning on line 6594. Documentation gap: AGENTS.md and architecture.md don't document the new handoff protocol.
